body:
## Context
The settings page (`settings.html` + `src/settings.js`) currently only exposes a display-name preference. Users have asked for a dark theme.

## Goal
Add a dark-mode toggle to the settings page that lets users switch the app between light and dark themes. The choice must persist across page loads and apply consistently on every page (home and settings).

## Acceptance criteria
- A labeled toggle control appears on the settings page under Preferences.
- Toggling it switches the page to a dark color scheme immediately (no reload).
- The preference is persisted (localStorage) and restored on load.
- The dark theme applies to all pages that load the shared stylesheet, not just settings.
- No reliance on external libraries; this is a tiny vanilla static app.

## Out of scope
- System (`prefers-color-scheme`) auto-detection — can be a follow-up.
- Per-component theming beyond the shared CSS variables.
title: Add a dark-mode toggle to the settings page
